name: Bicol Express
description: is the ultimate comfort food! With pork cubes cooked in coconut milk and chili peppers, it's rich, creamy, spicy and delicious.

name: Dinakdakan
description: is a popular Ilocano delicacy from the Philippines, specifically a savory, creamy, and tangy dish made from grilled pork and pork liver.

name: Dinuguan
description: which comes from the root word dugo (meaning "blood"), is a savory Filipino stew made of bite-sized pork cooked in pig's blood, vinegar, and spices, including garlic, onions, and chili peppers.

name: Igado
description: is a popular Ilocano dish , a savory stew made primarily with pork liver and other pork organ meats (kidney, heart) simmered in soy sauce, vinegar, and spices. With a vegetable (carrots, potato and red bellpepper).

name: Menudillo
description: a Filipino dish that is essentially a ground pork version of menudo or picadillo, featuring minced meat stewed in tomato sauce with diced potatoes, carrots, raisins

name: Menudo
description: a classic Filipino stew consisting of diced pork, pig liver, potatoes, carrots, and hotdogs simmered in a savory tomato-based sauce.

name: Pork Sinigang
description: (sinigang na baboy) is a popular Filipino comfort food soup known for its distinct savory and tart flavor. It is made by boiling pork (typically belly or ribs) with a souring agent—usually tamarind—along with vegetables like  radish and string beans.

name: Lechon Belly
description: Crispy rolled pork belly roasted to perfection with juicy, flavorful meat and crunchy golden skin, served Filipino-style for every special occasion.

name: Lechon Ulo
description: Traditional Filipino roasted pig head with tender, flavorful meat and irresistibly crispy skin, perfect for sharing and celebrations.

name: Whole Lechon
description: A classic Filipino centerpiece featuring a whole roasted pig with perfectly crispy skin and juicy, savory meat prepared for festive gatherings and celebrations.
